commands.review_summary:
The PR removes `contacts other delete`, deletes its generated docs and tests, and adds a changelog note explaining the unsupported Other Contacts delete behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. current main copies an `otherContacts/...` entry to a regular contact and deletes only the copied `people/...` resource, while the People API discovery surface exposes no Other Contacts delete method. The PR body also reports live account checks where the original Other Contacts remained present.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This collaborator-authored PR needs maintainer acceptance of the intentional CLI compatibility break; there is no narrow automated repair target unless maintainers choose the unsupported-stub path.

target.title: fix(contacts): remove nonfunctional other contact delete
target.body:
## Summary

- remove `contacts other delete`, which cannot delete Other Contacts through the public People API
- remove the copy-then-delete workaround that reported success while leaving the source entry unchanged
- remove obsolete tests and generated command docs
- document the compatibility break in the changelog

## Live proof

Using `clawdbot@gmail.com`, the command was tested against two test-owned Other Contacts:

- the existing implementation failed with 400 because its copy mask included fields forbidden for Other Contacts mutations
- after narrowing the mask to Google's allowed fields, the copy request and temporary regular-contact deletion succeeded
- both original `otherContacts/...` resources remained present after repeated list checks and propagation waits
- no regular-contact copy remained

The public People API exposes Other Contacts list, search, and copy operations, but no delete operation. Keeping the command would preserve a false success contract.

## Verification

- live `contacts other list` and `contacts other search`
- removed invocation exits usage 2
- generated docs contain only list/search
- focused contacts and dry-run tests
- structured autoreview: clean
- `make ci`
